description: Adversarial invariant for the hospital-information-system and electronic-health-record audit: broad clinical information systems should be grounded in computing/software/records lineage, not imaging modality, lab workflow, or later PKI security infrastructure.

checks.reason: X-ray imaging is one possible application served by HIS, not the platform prerequisite for broad HIS.
Laboratory diagnostics is one possible workflow served by HIS, not the broad software-system prerequisite.
Operational HIS implementations require electronic computing infrastructure.
HIS is implemented through coordinated software subsystems and application programs.
The current graph uses relational DBMS as the nearest database-management node, so the HIS edge must stay enabling rather than overclaiming a hard relational-model dependency.
HIS should stay anchored to the HELP operational chronology rather than the generated 1948 placeholder.
PKI is later secure-exchange infrastructure and should not be a prerequisite for early computer-based medical records.
EHRs digitize the older medical-record lineage rather than emerging from PKI.
Hospital information systems are deployment lineage and integration infrastructure for electronic records.
EHR should stay anchored to the Regenstrief Medical Record system operation date.
